content.problemStatement:
Chef has a pepperoni pizza in the shape of a N×NN \times NN×N grid; both its rows and columns are numbered 111 through NNN. Some cells of this grid have pepperoni on them, while some do not. Chef wants to cut the pizza vertically in half and give the two halves to two of his friends. Formally, one friend should get everything in the columns 111 through N/2N/2N/2 and the other friend should get everything in the columns N/2+1N/2+1N/2+1 through NNN.

Before doing that, if Chef wants to, he may choose one row of the grid and reverse it, i.e. swap the contents of the cells in the iii-th and N+1−iN+1-iN+1−i-th column in this row for each iii (1≤i≤N/21 \le i \le N/21≤i≤N/2).

After the pizza is cut, let's denote the number of cells containing pepperonis in one half by p1p_1p1​ and their number in the other half by p2p_2p2​. Chef wants to minimise their absolute difference. What is the minimum value of ∣p1−p2∣|p_1-p_2|∣p1​−p2​∣?
constraints: the sum of N⋅NN \cdot NN⋅N over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains a string with length NNN describing the iii-th row of the grid; this string contains only characters '1' (denoting a cell with pepperonis) and '0' (denoting a cell without pepperonis).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum absolute difference between the number of cells with pepperonis in the half-pizzas given to Chef's friends.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Initially, ∣p1−p2∣=4|p_1-p_2| = 4∣p1​−p2​∣=4, but if Chef reverses any one of the first four rows from "100000" to "000001", ∣p1−p2∣|p_1-p_2|∣p1​−p2​∣ becomes 222.
Example case 2: Initially, ∣p1−p2∣=0|p_1-p_2| = 0∣p1​−p2​∣=0. We cannot make that smaller by reversing any row.
